mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels increase inflation expectations, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums, which depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of instances when CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60d returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of UNRATE above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20d returns (positive returns >55% of the time), or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a rapid increase in the federal funds rate compresses equity valuations by raising discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5d returns in >60% of instances during tightening cycles, or the predictive relationship reverses when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate earnings growth, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or rising INDPRO coincides with negative 20d returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized, weak economic expansion reduces corporate profit expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60d returns in >60% of instances when GDPC1 is below 11500, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable federal funds rate indicates robust labor demand without aggressive monetary tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20d returns (negative returns >55% of the time), or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, simultaneous rebounds in industrial production and housing starts signal renewed economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The combined rebound signal yields negative 5d returns in >60% of instances during recovery phases, or the predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.
